original: Possible start of the first Byzantine indiction cycle
Northmen defeated near Louvaine, France
Baldwin VI becomes Count of Flanders
Ubaldo Allucingoli replaces Alexander III as Pope Lucius III
Königsberg (modern day Kaliningrad) founded by Teutonic Knights and named in honor of the Bohemian King Ottokar II
Rabbi Moses Ben Nachman establishes a Jewish community in Jerusalem
Tvrtko I writes in castro nostro Vizoka vocatum from old town Visoki
Tatars plunder Kyiv, Ukraine
Pieter Both sworn in as 1st Governor General of the Dutch East Indies
Vincent Fettmich expels Jews from Frankfurt-on-Main, Germany
-4] French queen-mother Maria de' Medici visits Amsterdam
French cardinal Mazarin and the Duke of Modena sign a treaty against Milan
35 Jews sentenced to life in prison in Lisbon Portugal
Dutch VOC ship Hofwegen struck by lighting and explodes off the coast of Batavia with six tons of silver aboard
Mission San Luis Obispo de Tolosa forms in California
2nd National Meeting in Hague
1st US bank robbery: the Bank of Pennsylvania at Carpenters' Hall in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania robbed of $162,821
Britain signs treaty with Nizam of Hyderabad, India
Bank of Manhattan Company opens in NYC (forerunner to Chase Manhattan)
Juno, one of the largest main belt asteroids discovered by German astronomer Karl Ludwig Harding.
1st colonies along Santa Fe Trail
Narcissa Whitman, one of the first white women to settle west of the Rocky Mountains, arrives at Walla Walla, Oregon Country (now US state of Washington)
Reconstruction begins on Synagogue of Rabbi Judah Hasid in Jerusalem
California Constitutional Convention convenes in Monterey
1st transatlantic cable fails after less than 1 month
Carrington Event: largest geomagnetic storm in recorded history. Richard Carrington and Richard Hodgson make 1st observation of a solar flare.
The first Pullman sleeping car is introduced on an overnight journey between Bloomington, Illinois, and Chicago
Grant assumes command of Federal forces at Cape Girardeau MI
Skirmishes at Boone Court House WV & Blue Creek WV
Battle at Chantilly (Ox Hill) Virginia (2100 casualties)
Federal income and excise taxes as outlined in the revenue Act of 1862 begin to be collected
6th Ohio Cavalry ambush at Barbees Crossroads Virginia
Federal troops reconquer Fort Smith Arkansas
Rail & ferry connection between San Francisco & Oakland inaugurated
2nd day of battle at Jonesboro Georgia, about 3,000 casualties
Skirmish at Hood evacuated confederates from Atlanta GA
Last Navaho chief Manuelito turns himself in at Fort Wingate
Cetshwayo ascends to the throne as king of the Zulu nation following the death of his father Mpande.
28th US Postmaster General: Marshall Jewell of Connecticut takes office
Sydney General Post Office opens in Australia
A murder conviction effectively forces the violent Irish anti-owner coal miners, the "Molly Maguires", to disband
First female telephone operator starts work, Emma Nutt, for the Edwin Holmes Telephone Dispatch Company in Boston
The Netherlands New Code of Criminal Law is enforced
Dutch Amateur Photography Cooperation established
Dutch Railway Deventer-Almelo opens
Brooklyn Bridegrooms win 3 MLB games in one day‚ feasting on the Pittsburgh Alleghenys 10- 9‚ 3-2‚ and 8-4, at Washington Park in Brooklyn
Gladstones' Second Home Rule Bill for Ireland passes in the House of Commons but vetoed by The House of Lords by 419 votes to 41
Wildfire destroys 350,000 acres, including the town of Hinckley, Minnesota, killing over 418 people
Boston subway opens, the first underground rapid transit system in North America
First forestry school in America opens at Biltmore Estate, North Carolina
Construction begins on NY Stock Exchange
British New Guinea placed under Australian administration
Canadian province of Alberta adopts Mountain Standard Time
New York Highlanders win their 6th consecutive MLB game in 3 days from Washington Senators; sweep AL record 3 straight double headers
Philadelphia beats the Red Sox 4-1 in 24 innings in Boston in the longest game in AL baseball history; both starters go the distance as A's hurler Jack Coombs overcomes Boston's Joe Harris
The International Federation of Intellectual Property Attorneys (FICPI) is established
French aviator Alexandre Fourny sets a world aircraft duration record of 11 hours, 1 minute, and 29 seconds, covering 720 km in a Maurice Farman biplane
France's Adolphe Pégoud performs the first inverted flight, using a Blériot model XI monoplane
Lord Kitchenerarrives in Paris
St Petersburg, Russia changes name to Petrograd
The last passenger pigeon, a female named Martha, dies in captivity in the Cincinnati Zoo in Ohio
Jerome Travers establishes a US Amateur Golf Championship record by downing George Crump, 14 & 13, in a 1st-round match at the Country Club of Detroit in Grosse Pointe Farms, Michigan

The German ambassador to the US pledges again that German submarines will no longer sink liners without warning and providing safety of passengers and crew following the sinking of the British liner "Arabic"
Annie Besant founds the India Home Rule League
Bulgaria declares war on Romania
US Keating-Owen Act bans child labor banned from interstate commerce
Battle of Megiddo starts, the final Allied offense during Sinai and Palestine campaign won by the Allies
US troops land in Vladivostok, Siberia, stay until 1920
Communist Party of America organizes in Chicago, Illinois after a schism with the Socialist Party of America
France creates Greater Lebanon
New town hall of Rotterdam opens
New York City law requires all "pool" rooms to change their name to "billiards", per article 31 of the state penal code approved in June
A magnitude 7.9 earthquake strikes Tokyo and Yokohama in Japan, killing 142,000 people
Kenchoji Rinzai temple in Kamakur Japan, heavily damaged by earthquake
Pierre de Coubertin steps down as chairman of International Olympic Committee; replaced by Henri de Baillet-Latour of Belgium
Turkey allows civil marriage
Albania becomes a kingdom, with Zogu I as king
NY World reports disappearance of supreme court justice Joseph Crater
NYC Mayor James J. "Gentleman Jimmy" Walker resigns due to graft charges

US Navy renames NAS Sunnyvale, near San Francisco, California,  NAS Moffett Field, in honor of Rear Admiral William A. Moffett; Moffett championed the construction of the base, but died in the crash of airship USS Akron before the base opened
SMJK Sam Tet was founded by Father Fourgs from the St. Michael Church, Ipoh, Perak, Malaysia.
Spelling-Marchand Laws enforced
Belgian boxer Gustave Roth beats Heinz Lazek of Austria in a 15 round points decision to win the EBU and vacant IBU world light heavyweight title in Vienna
4th Chicago College All-Star Game: All-Stars beat Green Bay, 6-0; 84,560 at Soldier Field
Battle of Gijon in Spain begins
General George Marshall sworn in as the United States Army Chief of Staff
Scientific journal "Physical Review" publishes 1st paper to deal with "black holes"
Swedish Prime Minister Per Albin Hansson declares neutrality in World War II
Switzerland mobilizes its forces and the Swiss Parliament elects Henri Guisan to head the Swiss Army (an event that can happen only during war or mobilization)
Switzerland proclaims neutrality at the outbreak of WWII
The Wound Badge for Wehrmacht, SS, Kriegsmarine, and Luftwaffe soldiers is instituted. The final version of the Iron Cross was also instituted on this date
Jews living in Germany are required to wear a yellow Star of David
German troops land on Taman peninsula
US Federal judge upholds detention of Japanese-Americans
Bulgaria government of Bagrjanow, resigns
Philadelphia Phillies outfielder Vince DiMaggio smashes his 4th grand slam of season, tying NL record, in an 8-3 win v Boston Braves
Greece votes for monarchy
Infielder Jack Lohrke hits NY Giants' 183rd homer of season in 2-1 win v Boston Braves; breaks NY Yankees MLB mark of 182 set in 1936
With a victory by Armed in the Washington Park Handicap, Calumet Farm becomes the first stable to surpass $1 million in annual earnings; leads all owners with total earnings of $1,402,436
Communist form North China People's Republic
1st network detective series "Martin Kane, Private Eye" debuts on NBC, airing live
Finnish long distance runner Viljo Heino runs 10,000m world record 29:27.2 in Kouvola, Finland
KMTV TV channel 3 in Omaha, Nebraska (CBS/NBC) begins broadcasting
13 North Korean divisions open assault on UN lines
West Berlin granted a constitution
US, Australia and New Zealand sign the ANZUS mutual defense treaty
Sutro Baths, San Francisco purchased by George Whitney
Willem Drees forms his 3rd Dutch government
101°F highest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in Sept
Buck Baker takes lead with 10 laps remaining to win the Southern 500 at Darlington in the most competitive event in NASCAR Grand National history; four drivers swap lead a record 35 times
Fokker begins building F-27 Fokker Friendship
WNOK (now WLTX) TV channel 19 in Columbia, SC (CBS) 1st broadcast
WTCN (now KARE) TV channel 11 in Minneapolis-St Paul, MN (MET) begins
Hurricane Carol strikes Long Island and New England, kills 68

Paul "Bear" Bryant opens the infamous 10-day football mini-camp in Junction, Texas; the ordeal achieves legendary status, becoming the subject of the 2001 book "The Junction Boys" and a television movie of the same name
2 Egyptian fighters shot down over Israel
KARD (now KSNW) TV channel 3 in Wichita, KS (NBC) begins broadcasting
Indian state of Tripura becomes a territory
KELP (now KCOS) TV channel 13 in El Paso, TX (PBS) begins broadcasting
Excursion train crashed into a ravine killing 175, injuring over 700, in Kendal, Jamaica
WAVY TV channel 10 in Portsmouth-Norfolk, VA (NBC) begins broadcasting
WHC (now WPXI) TV channel 11 in Pittsburgh, PA (NBC) 1st broadcast
WTLV TV channel 12 in Jacksonville, FL (ABC/NBC) begins broadcasting
St Louis Cardinals pitcher Vinegar Bend Mizell walks a NL record 9 batters in a 1-0 shutout of Cincinnati Reds
German sprinter Armin Hary wins the coveted 100m gold medal in 10.2 at the Rome Olympics; first non-American to win the event since 1928
US 4 x 100m medley relay team swims world record 4:05.4 to beat Australia and win the gold medal at the Rome Olympics; Frank McKinney, Paul Hait, Lance Larson & Jeff Farrell
US 4 x 200m freestyle relay team of George Harrison, Dick Blick, Mike Troy & Jeff Farrell swim world record 8:10.2 to beat Japan by 3.3 seconds and win gold medal at the Rome Olympics
1st conference of neutral countries held in Belgrade
The Eritrean War of Independence officially begins with the shooting of the Ethiopian police by Hamid Idris Awate
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
12,000 die in an earthquake in western Iran
Channel Television launches to 54,000 households in the Channel Islands.
KATC TV channel 3 in Lafayette, LA (ABC) begins broadcasting
United Nations announces Earth's population has hit 3 billion
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
Cliff Richardand the Shadows 1st British rock group to perform in Israel
Language laws in Belgium goes into effect causing a riot
Walter Kennedy begins his tenure as the commissioner of the NBA, succeeding Maurice Podoloff
SF Giants relieverMasanori Murakamibecomes the first Japanese-born player to appear in US MLB; on debut, the Osuki native throws a scoreless inning in a 4-1 loss v New York Mets
Indo-Pakistani conflict - Pakistani counter-attack (Operation Grand Slam,)
KIFW (now KTNL) TV channel 13 in Sitka, AK (CBS) begins broadcasting
Cincinnati Reds reliever Bob Lee walks Dick Groat with bases loaded in 21st inning to give SF Giants a 1-0 win at Crosley Field; 20 scoreless innings ties MLB mark, Pirates v Braves 1918
KMNE TV channel 7 in Bassett, NB (PBS) begins broadcasting
WIRT TV channel 13 in Hibbing, MN (ABC) begins broadcasting
WJRJ (WTCG, WTBS) TV channel 17 in Atlanta, GA (IND) begins
Earthquake destroys Ferdows Persia, 2,000 killed
Pirate Radio Marina (Netherlands) begins transmitting
Jerry Lewis' 4th Muscular Dystrophy telethon
Failed assassination attempt on Jordanian king Hussain
Velasco Ibarra re-elected as President of Ecuador
Rolling Stones sue manager Allen Klein
The Irish Republican Army set off a series of bombs across Northern Ireland injuring a number of people
An American sweep of the medals in the women's 200m butterfly at the Munich Olympics; Karen Moe takes gold in world record 2:15.57 ahead of teammates Lynn Colella and Ellie Daniel
Australian swimmerShane Gouldwins the 200m freestyle in world record 2:03.56 at the Munich Olympics; her 3rd gold medal & 3rd world record of the Games
Egypt & Libya form federation
Japanese gymnast Sawao Kato wins the parallel bars gold medal at the Munich Olympics, his 3rd gold of the Games including team & individual all-round victories
Polish shooter Józef Zapędzki successfully defends his Olympic 25m rapid fire pistol title at the Munich Games with an Olympic record 595 points
Valeriy Borzov of the Soviet Union runs 10.14 to beat American Robert Miller and Lennox Miller of Jamaica in the coveted men's 100m final at the Munich Olympics
Welshman Richard Meade representing Great Britain and riding Laurieston grabs the equestrian individual and team 3-day event gold medals at the Munich Olympics
74-year-old Hafnia Hotel burns, killing 35 (Copenhagen, Denmark)

Gloucestershire beats Sussex by 40 runs in the Gillette Cup cricket final at Lord's to win their first major trophy since the Championship in 1877; South African all-rounder Mike Procter stars with 94 and 2 for 37
Libya nationalizes 51 percent of nine other oil companies' concessions
Dutch law against pirate radio goes into effect
The SR-71 Blackbird sets the record for flying from New York to London in 1 hour, 54 minutes, and 56.4 seconds
Train accident at Zagreb Yugoslavia, 121 killed
5 Protestant civilians are killed and 7 were wounded in a Provisional Irish Republican Army gun attack on Tullyvallen Orange Hall near Newtownhamilton, County Armagh
All political parties forbidden in Bangladesh
French navigatorJacques Cartierreaches Hochelaga (Montreal)
Holy Roman EmperorCharles Vdemands creation of an Imperial League (German state)
Spanish KingPhilip IIreceives sacraments
Battle at Castelnaudary: Henri de Montmorency's rebel army loses to French royalist forces loyal toLouis XIII
KingLouis XIVof France dies after a reign of 72 years, the longest of any major European monarch
Catherine IIof Russia endorses Ivan Betskoy's plans for a Foundling Home in Moscow
"Poems on Various Subjects, Religious and Moral" by American slavePhillis Wheatleypublished in the UK - first known book of poetry published by a Black woman
Mozartpublishes his 6th String Quartet, Opus 10 in Vienna
Aaron Burracquitted of charges of plotting to set up an empire
Construction begins on the Grand Central Depot forCornelius Vanderbilt's New York and Harlem Railroad (later replaced by Grand Central Station, and then by Grand Central Terminal)
French EmperorNapoleon IIIsurrenders to Prussian army and taken prisoner at the Battle of Sedan, France
Lord Kitchener's troops attack Omdurman, Sudan
Cumann na nGaedheal (Irish Council) founded byArthur Griffithin order to promote a buy Irish campaign
Wilfrid Laurieroversees Alberta and Saskatchewan joining the Confederation of Canada as its 8th and 9th provinces, both separated from the Northwest Territories
Anthony Fokkerfamously flies his aircraft "The Spider" around the tower of the Grote Kerk in Haarlem, the Netherlands
George Bernard Shaw's play "Androcles and the Lion" premieres in London
Zhang Xun's Wuwei Corps captures Nanjing on behalf of EmperorYuan Shikaiin the Republic of China's Second Revolution, ending Chinese independence and causing Sun Yat Sen to flee to Japan
Frank Wedekind's play "Herakles" premieres in Munich

Nederlander Theatre opens at 208 W 41 St NYC, designed by William Neil Smith for theatrical impresario Walter C. Jordan, originally named the National Theatre, it was also known as theBilly Rose(1959-78), and the Trafalgar (1978-80)
Future Baseball Hall of Fame 1st basemanLou Gehrighits his 3rd grand slam in 4 days & 6th homer in consecutive games in NY Yankees' 5-1 win v Boston Red Sox
Benito Mussolinicancels civil rights of Italian Jews
Adolf Hitlerorders extermination of mentally ill through the "Aktion T4 Euthanasia Program," arguing that wartime "was the best time for the elimination of the incurably ill"
In the "scoop of the century," Telegraph journalistClare Hollingworthbecomes the first to report the outbreak of World War II
Chiang Kai-shekagain becomes Chairman of the Nationalist Government of China
KingGeorge VIpromotes Montgomery to field marshal
Prime MinisterDavid Ben-Gurionorders establishment of Israeli secret service Mossad
Paramount Pictures releases "Rear Window", directed byAlfred Hitchcockand starringJames StewartandGrace Kelly
ColonelMuammar Gaddafideposes King Idris in the Libyan Revolution

Pittsburgh Pirates start what is believed to be first all-black line-up (including several Latinos) in MLB history‚ in 10-7 win v Philadelphia Phillies; includes future Hall of FamersRoberto ClementeandWillie Stargell
In his first title defenseGeorge Foremanretains his WBC & WBA heavyweight crowns when he KOs Jose "King" Roman of Puerto Rico in round 1 in Tokyo, Japan
Pennsylvania's new State House bell (known today as the Liberty Bell) arrives in Philadelphia from Whitechapel Foundry in London, England
World War II starts when Germany invades Poland by attacking the Free City of Danzig

American chess grandmasterBobby Fischerdefeats Russian champion Boris Spassky 12.5-8.5 in Reykjavik, Iceland, in the most publicized world title match ever played; Fischer becomes the first American to win the title
